pr_summary: Automated review found 16 issue(s) across security, correctness, performance, style, and test coverage.

findings.line_content: +export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
findings.title: IDOR
findings.description: Sensitive operation performed using externally supplied identifiers without ownership verification.
findings.suggestion: Verify that the requesting user owns the resource before processing the operation.
findings.evidence: export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {

findings.title: IDOR
findings.description: Sensitive operation performed using externally supplied identifiers without ownership verification.
findings.suggestion: Verify that the requesting user owns the resource before processing the operation.
findings.evidence: export async function bulkCancelOrders(orderIds: string[]): Promise<void> {

findings.title: IDOR
findings.description: Order cancellation performed using externally supplied identifiers without checking ownership.
findings.suggestion: Check ownership of the order before cancellation.

findings.title: Infinite Polling Loop
findings.description: Loop may run indefinitely if status never changes.
findings.suggestion: Add timeout, retry limits, or cancellation logic.

findings.title: Repeated Polling Delay
findings.description: Polling with fixed delays can consume resources and increase latency.
findings.suggestion: Use event-driven updates, backoff strategies, or webhooks.

findings.title: Potential Large Collection Iteration
findings.description: Looping over large collections may become expensive.

findings.title: Sequential Async Processing
findings.description: Operations are executed sequentially and may become slow at scale.
findings.suggestion: Use Promise.all() or parallel execution when safe.

findings.title: Sequential awaits inside loop
findings.description: await sleep(1000) is used inside a loop without Promise.all, causing sequential execution of database/network operations.
findings.suggestion: Use Promise.all to execute database/network operations concurrently.

findings.title: Missing Order Null Check
findings.description: order may be null or undefined before accessing its properties.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to verify that order exists before use.

findings.title: Potential NaN Calculation
findings.description: Discount code may not exist in map.
findings.suggestion: Validate discount code or provide fallback.

findings.title: Magic Number
findings.suggestion: Extract the value into a named constant.

findings.title: Overuse of Generic Types
findings.description: The discounts object is explicitly typed as 'any', bypassing TypeScript type safety.
findings.suggestion: Define a proper type or interface for the discounts map.
findings.evidence: const discounts: any = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 }

findings.line_content: +export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
findings.title: Missing Order Cancellation Tests
findings.description: Order cancellation flow lacks critical validation tests.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests for: 1) already cancelled order, 2) invalid order ID, 3) permission denied (unauthorized), and 4) notification failure.
findings.evidence: export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {

findings.title: Missing State Transition Tests
findings.description: Status updates require validation of state transitions.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests verifying valid and invalid state transitions.

findings.line_content: +export function calculateDiscount(price: number, discountCode: string): number {
findings.title: Missing Discount Validation Tests
findings.description: Discount calculation logic may fail for invalid or expired codes.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests checking: 1) invalid discount code, 2) expired discount code, and 3) unsupported discount code.
findings.evidence: export function calculateDiscount(price: number, discountCode: string): number {

findings.title: Missing Notification Failure Tests
findings.description: notificationService.send on line 18 does not handle cases where notification fails.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests checking: 1) notification service returns an error, and 2) notification service returns a success response.

missing_tests: Add unit tests checking: 1) notification service returns an error, and 2) notification service returns a success response.
Add unit tests checking: 1) invalid discount code, 2) expired discount code, and 3) unsupported discount code.
Add unit tests verifying valid and invalid state transitions.
Add unit tests for: 1) already cancelled order, 2) invalid order ID, 3) permission denied (unauthorized), and 4) notification failure.
